items.title: The Rosetta Stone
items.description: A granodiorite stele inscribed with three versions of a decree issued in Memphis, Egypt. It was the key to deciphering Egyptian hieroglyphs.

items.title: Mona Lisa
items.description: A half-length portrait painting by Italian artist Leonardo da Vinci. Considered an archetypal masterpiece of the Italian Renaissance.

items.title: The Great Wave off Kanagawa
items.description: A woodblock print by the Japanese ukiyo-e artist Hokusai. It is Hokusai's most famous work and is one of the most recognizable works of Japanese art in the world.

items.title: Bust of Nefertiti
items.description: A painted stucco-coated limestone bust of Nefertiti, the Great Royal Wife of Egyptian Pharaoh Akhenaten. It is one of the most copied works of ancient Egypt.

items.title: Girl with a Pearl Earring
items.description: An oil painting by Dutch Golden Age painter Johannes Vermeer. It is a tronie of a girl with a headscarf and a pearl earring.

items.title: Terracotta Army Chariot
items.description: A collection of terracotta sculptures depicting the armies of Qin Shi Huang, the first Emperor of China. It is a form of funerary art buried with the emperor.

items.title: Starry Night
items.description: An oil-on-canvas painting by the Dutch Post-Impressionist painter Vincent van Gogh. Painted in June 1889, it depicts the view from the east-facing window of his asylum room at Saint-Rémy-de-Provence.

items.title: The Thinker
items.description: A bronze sculpture by Auguste Rodin, usually placed on a stone pedestal. The work depicts a nude male figure of over life-size sitting on a rock with his chin resting on one hand.

items.title: Gutenberg Bible
items.description: The first major book printed in Europe using mass-produced movable metal type. It marked the start of the 'Gutenberg Revolution' and the age of printed books in the West.

items.title: Mechanical Planetary Clock
items.description: A complex mechanical clock designed to show the positions of the planets in the solar system as well as the time.

items.title: Viking Helmet
items.description: A rare iron helmet from the Viking Age, found in Norway. It features a distinctive eye-guard and was a status symbol for warriors.

items.title: Japanese Samurai Armor
items.description: A complete suit of Samurai armor (Yoroi) featuring detailed lacquered metal plates and silk cord bindings.

items.title: The Astronomer's Globe
items.description: A terrestrial globe showing the latest geographical discoveries of the 16th century, crafted by the famous cartographer Gerardus Mercator.

items.title: Ancient Greek Amphora
items.description: A black-figure pottery vessel used for storing wine, depicting scenes from the Iliad. A masterpiece of Attic pottery.

items.title: Aztec Sun Stone
items.description: A massive basalt sculpture representing the Aztec view of the cosmos and the cyclical nature of time.

items.title: Medieval Manuscript
items.description: An illuminated manuscript featuring gold leaf and vibrant pigments, containing prayers and botanical illustrations.

items.title: The Marble Faun
items.description: A graceful marble sculpture of a resting satyr, a testament to the skill of Hellenistic sculptors.

items.title: Antique Telescope
items.description: A brass achromatic refractor telescope used by early modern astronomers to chart the stars.

items.title: Renaissance Lute
items.description: A stringed musical instrument with a vaulted body, widely used in the courtly music of the 16th century.

items.title: Art Nouveau Stained Glass
items.description: An intricate glass panel featuring organic floral motifs, exemplifying the elegance of the Art Nouveau movement.
